So this is actually a video that we made with Tom following this exact structure and what I want to show you, what we're getting into it is that it's really hitting the concepts of each of those things. It doesn't have to be so scripted that it's like, you might be watching this because of this. And if you're feeling this, you know what I mean? So you have to make it feel organic and if you're making multiple of these then you want to hit all of those feelings, right? You want to hit all of those milestones but the difference is between, because otherwise you have one perfect ad, it would always work, right? And that's not how life works. And so there is a degree of creativity that has to be made here and that is the skill of marketing. So I want to break this down and show you how this follows the structure. We lead in with a question here instead but everything else is more or less the same. Okay, quick question ladies. What are you going to look like in six weeks? But you can see that we hit about a minute and 22 seconds. So that's the kind of length that you're looking for for this. Now, mind you, this is not perfect. That was our rendition of that style video. If you were to blend, if I were to do this again, this was done, we made this a year ago. I would probably have quickly covered point one and two and then maybe point three. I would have dug a little bit deeper into a tactical way that we hold people accountable, or I would have made the nutrition like one specific style of nutrition so that there's a little bit of a belief that's being broken with the food, right? And so or a belief that's being broken in the fitness. So you can have three, but highlight one of them. And that's the one we want to deconstruct and really like provide something tactically useful. This video, I don't think provide anything tactically useful besides the simple three structure layout. And so the belief would be that they had to have all three. So that's the primary belief that this one's going after. But if I were to do this again, and I wanted to make variations of it to create multiple video ads that we're going to convert, I would break down more into the one of those three that and then driven that home a little bit harder for maybe another 15 seconds. That is what I would have done for feedback on this for us. But this video obviously did really well. It's still converted. And so I wanted to explain this to you as a pure educational example. What I wanted to also take the opportunity to do was show you what I look at. Probably the jigomeys that I look the most at in terms of looking at what's working in marketing right now. So my friends, Brandon and Katelyn, they've been good friends of ours for a long time. They were at our wedding. If you look at info and ads and you want to see the styles of things that are working right now, now mind you some of their stuff are straight webinars, which are like 22 minutes long. That's not what you want to do. Katelyn is also an amazing pitch person. You know, one of the best in the world for fitness. So like she's obviously very, very good at this. But you'll notice that she purposely does not transcribe her videos. And I actually asked about this. I was like, why don't you guys transcribe the videos? And he was like, Katelyn is just so engaging in video that we want them to start listening to her. And as soon as they click in, he's like, we've got them. And so I want to underline the fact that I started this whole piece on the educate style videos. You have to be good on camera. You have to be funny. You have to have character. You have to be dynamic to get people to want to continue to listen. And so you'll notice though, really, really short copy and then they let the video do the talking. And so really easy call to action here. And so if you look at these actual, this was a 22 minute drop, but this one is a two minute video. This one's three minutes. Like you can see here, but you don't need to make super, super polished ads obviously for them to work. And so I'm just showing this to you two and a half minutes. So that's like you have a little bit of leeway. She's just incredibly, you can already see her personality and how she's talking like she's very engaging on video. So that is the first one that I would look at for women and looking at the ad styles and even the topics that are working well. The second one is for guys and they run their ads from this guy. So if you look at this, they're all about ketone intermittent fasting. So they've understood the marketing side that those are the they're trying to break one or two beliefs around that. They used to only talk about intermittent fasting. I see far more about keto now in their marketing. And that's probably because it's a more compelling like don't eat sugar, eat butter and bacon and blah, blah, blah. So they're taking keto to the mass market. We've been around long enough that we don't think it's anything special, but for mass market, it's like new and revolutionary still. So obviously this is one that's a very professional video. But if you look the same copy style exists, it's short copy straight to a page. And if you're looking at what's being covered, so this is where you want to. So he's breaking one specific belief, which is that in all the videos, his one belief is that you need to stop eating sugar and that fat is really, really good for you. Right. And that's see, stop eating sugar. You know, now is that true? No. But if you're trying to reach mass market, then you need to have some sort of hook that's going to bring them in. And then obviously you can educate them further on things that matter. Right. So it's when they see debunking myths, it's the same as breaking beliefs. And so you can come from that angle because people love myth busters, people love debunking myths. And so if you can do that, you're automatically positioning yourself as an authority on the topic, which I'll get to in the next thing of why these tend to work well.
